title: melaleuca clavifolia
intro: Melaleuca clavifolia, also known as the club-leaved paperbark or coastal tea-tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its essential oil, which is rich in caryophyllene and limonene. The health benefits of Melaleuca clavifolia include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Melaleuca clavifolia are often used topically in the form of creams, ointments, and tinctures to promote skin health and alleviate various skin conditions.
